system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:

vertices
(

    (-50  25  25)   // 0
    (-50  25 -25)   // 1
    (-50 -25 -25)   // 2
    (-50 -25  25)   // 3

    (  0  25  25)   // 4
    (  0  25 -25)   // 5
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 6
    (  0 -25  25)   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend section
    // Vertices 8-11
    ( 25   0  25)   // 8
    ( 25   0 -25)   // 9
    ( 25 -50 -25)   // 10
    ( 25 -50  25)   // 11

    // Block 2 - outlet straight duct
    // Vertices 12-15
    ( 25  -75  25)   // 12
    ( 25  -75 -25)   // 13
    ( 25 -125 -25)   // 14
    ( 25 -125  25)   // 15

    // Block 3 - extended outlet duct
    // Vertices 16-19
    (-500  -75  25)   // 16
    (-500  -75 -25)   // 17
    (-500 -125 -25)   // 18
    (-500 -125  25)   // 19
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight duct
    hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend section
    hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Post-bend straight duct
    hex (11 12 13 10 3 15 14 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 3: Extended outlet duct
    hex (12 16 17 13 10 18 19 14) (40 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 17 18 19)
        );
    }

    walls
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 walls
            (0 4 5 1)
            (3 7 6 2)
            (0 4 7 3)
            (1 5 6 2)

            // Block 1 walls
            (7 8 9 6)
            (3 11 10 2)
            (7 11 10 6)
            (8 9 2 3)

            // Block 2 walls
            (11 12 13 10)
            (3 15 14 2)
            (11 15 14 10)
            (12 13 2 3)

            // Block 3 walls
            (12 16 17 13)
            (10 18 19 14)
            (12 16 19 14)
            (13 17 18 10)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

{

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block is "inside-out"

The primary error is:
```
Block hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) ... is inside-out
```

This means the vertex ordering in the hex blocks does not follow the right-hand rule convention required by OpenFOAM. When viewed from outside the block, the vertices should go around the bottom face counterclockwise and then the top face counterclockwise.

### Root Cause

Looking at the current block definitions:

**Block 0:** `hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2)` — This ordering is incorrect.
**Block 1:** `hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2)`
**Block 2:** `hex (11 12 13 10 3 15 14 2)`
**Block 3:** `hex (12 16 17 13 10 18 19 14)`

Additionally, the **boundary face definitions** also need to be consistent with the new block topology after fixing the hex ordering.

### Recommended Solution

Redefine the blocks so that each hex definition follows the correct winding order. For a hex block `(v0 v1 v2 v3 v4 v5 v6 v7)`, the first four vertices form one face and the last four form the opposite face, with proper handedness.

For this specific geometry where the bend turns in the **-y direction**, the blocks should be reordered as follows:

- **Block 0**: `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` — change from `(0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2)` to standard winding
- **Block 1**: `hex (7 6 10 11 3 2 9 8)` — reverse the original
- **Block 2**: `hex (11 10 14 13 3 2 15 12)` — reverse the original
- **Block 3**: `hex (12 13 17 16 2 14 19 18)` — reverse the original

Then update **all boundary face definitions** to match the new block vertex ordering. Every face in the `walls` boundary must be updated to reflect the new block connectivity.

---

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cannot find "points"

This is a cascading error. Since `blockMesh` failed, no mesh was generated, so `rhoSimpleFoam` cannot find the mesh files (`points`, `faces`, `cells`). Once `blockMesh` is fixed and run successfully, this error will be resolved automatically.

---

## Additional Issue: Boundary Name Mismatches

After fixing the blockMeshDict, the boundary patches referenced in the `0/` field files may not exist. The current `blockMeshDict` defines these patches:

- `inlet`
- `outlet`
- `walls`

However, looking at the `0/` files (T, U, p), there are references to `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`. These need to either be added to `blockMeshDict` or replaced with the existing patch names (`walls`) in the boundary condition files.

**Recommended approach:** Keep the `blockMeshDict` boundary names as `inlet`, `outlet`, and `walls`. Then update the `0/` field files (T, U, p) to use `walls` instead of `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`.

---

## Summary of Actions

1. **Fix `blockMeshDict`**: Correct the vertex ordering for all four hex blocks to eliminate the "inside-out" error.
2. **Update boundary faces**: Ensure all boundary face definitions in `blockMeshDict` are consistent with the corrected block topology.
3. **Fix `0/` field files**: Replace `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall` with `walls` in T, U, and p files (or add the missing patches to blockMeshDict).
4. **Re-run `blockMesh`** before running `rhoSimpleFoam`.</reviewer_analysis>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
